extraction.one_line_summary: BILBAO Pello won Stage 3 by attacking on the final corkscrew climb, edging YATES Simon on the line after a fast descent.
tactical_patterns: BILBAO prioritized stage win over time gains, sprinting final 300m while GC rivals Yates/Hindley focused on position.
HONORÉ Miguel breakaway launched day before birthday suggests EF-Education deliberately burns domestiques early on stage profiles favoring them.
Alpecin-Deceuninck contested intermediate sprint with GROVES suggesting team prioritizes points opportunities even on summit finishes.
VINE Jay maximized KOM points but didn't follow final attack, revealing climbers classification prioritized over GC ambitions.

rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: positive: Fast descending skills post-summit allowed him to rejoin leaders after slower ascent pace, tactical versatility undervalued
rider_signals.DENNIS Rowan: negative: Back of peloton and bike change suggests mechanical or crash-related weakness on critical stage
rider_signals.HONORÉ Miguel: neutral: EF-Education released him to DSM, now beating them in breakaway selections, questionable team retention decision
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: Second place but tactical focus on GC time over stage win cost him victory by one second
rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: Deliberately riding for KOM jersey over GC, clear role definition within UAE team hierarchy
